title: Dracaena caboverdeana
taxonomy: Dracaena caboverdeana is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Asparagaceae, subfamily Nolinoideae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plant species. It is also sometimes classified under the former family Ruscaceae, but the updated classification places it within the Asparagaceae family. The species is further categorized under the order Asparagales and class Liliopsida. The precise classification of Dracaena caboverdeana reflects its unique characteristics and position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Dracaena caboverdeana, also known as the Cape Verde dragon tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae, native to the Cape Verde Islands. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Dracaena caboverdeana include saponins, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dracaena caboverdeana is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including coughs, colds, and digestive issues.
